cves.summary: Studio 5000 Logix Designer uses a hardcoded cryptographic key; an unauthenticated remote attacker can bypass verification and connect to Logix controllers (incl. ControlLogix 1756).
Logix controllers store the user-facing textual program separately from executed logic, letting an attacker modify running logic while the original code is still displayed.
SIMATIC S7-1200/S7-1500 CPUs have a memory-protection bypass allowing an unauthenticated remote attacker to read/write protected memory or execute code.
